goal: Phase 1 trigger-first revision-first local state model for Orun
notes: Task 0004 (M2 PR-B — internal/statestore real CompareAndSwap + List + 100-goroutine atomicity / exclusivity property suite + pgregory.net/rapid path-alphabet round-trip) verified PASS and squash-merged via PR #155 → main commit 0fa2111 on 2026-05-30. impl/task-0004-m2-statestore-prb branch deleted. Verifier report at ai/reports/task-0004-verifier.md (Result: PASS, no blocking issues, no spec proposals). Coverage on internal/statestore measured 95.4 % (gate ≥ 95 %). Local quality gates (build, vet, -race test, make test-state-redesign, kiox -- orun validate / plan --changed / run --dry-run) all green. Required CI runs 26670829548 (CI / Orun Plan, real plan invocation 0×3→0 jobs) + 26670829550 (Harness dry-run guard, full [guard] PASS battery) reviewed at log level.

M2 contract on the local driver is now CLOSED for non-typed surface: frozen StateStore interface (Root, Read, Write, CreateIfAbsent, CompareAndSwap, List, Delete) with full atomicity guarantees per state-store.md §3 — Write temp+fsync+rename with EXDEV cross-device fallback, CreateIfAbsent O_EXCL, CompareAndSwap with per-path sync.Mutex narrowing the in-process race (additive, §6 permits 'best-effort on local'), List skipping symlinks + .orun-tmp-* and rejecting alphabet escapes via paths.go ValidatePath, Delete no-op-on-absent + refuses (empty + non-empty) directories with ErrInvalid, orphan-tempfile sweep at construction.

Verifier non-blocking findings on 0004 (carried forward, none blocking): per-path sync.Mutex is in-process only (irrelevant to local Phase 1 single-process semantics; remote driver Phase 2 will supersede with native conditional updates); empty-directory Delete returns ErrInvalid (state-store.md §3.4 only mandates non-empty — slightly stricter, documented since Task 0003); coverage 95.4 % satisfies gate but sits below the 96 % stretch target — PR-C will add refs.go/indexes.go and should lift it.

Next task = Task 0005 = M2 PR-C: typed refs reader/writer helpers (refs.go covering data-model.md §6.1–§6.4 — LatestRevisionRef, LatestExecutionRef, TriggerRef, NamedRef) + typed index writers (indexes.go covering §7.1 RevisionIndexEntry, §7.2 ExecutionIndexEntry) + RebuildIndexes() stub returning ErrInvalid-wrapped 'rebuild deferred to M3+'. Strict constraints: zero string concatenation for paths (everything via paths.go), deterministic JSON via MarshalIndent('','  ') with trailing \n + no HTML escaping, CAS helpers take *ObjectMeta from prior read (no re-read inside helper), index writers use CreateIfAbsent (re-write → ErrExists), package stays leaf-clean, NO production-caller wiring (cmd/orun, internal/state, internal/runner, internal/runbundle untouched), no new error sentinels, no new path helpers in paths.go (if missing → write proposal, do not add silently). Coverage gate stays ≥95 %; PR-C should leave it ≥96 %. Tests cover round-trip, ErrNotFound, ErrExists on duplicate index write, CAS exactly-one-wins on LatestRevisionRef, and JSON byte-for-byte stability via internal/testfx/statefs.AssertJSONFile.

After Task 0005 lands and is verified, Milestone M2 is complete (the implementation-plan.md M2 'Done when' checklist is satisfied: ≥95 % coverage, atomicity tests, concurrent CreateIfAbsent N=100 — already shipped in PR-B; public docs on every exported symbol — required cross-cutting). Next milestone = M3 (internal/revision: PlanRevision model, RevisionKey + collision suffix, WriteRevision executing the ordered writes from design.md §5.1, manifest helpers, ResolveRevision with the seven resolution branches, .orun/version.json on first creation). M3 depends on M1 + M2.

Persistent local environment quirk (NOT a regression): kiox -- orun plan --changed --intent examples/intent.yaml occasionally fails on composition-cache resolution (stack.yaml at ~/.orun/cache/compositions/c41fc08… has no spec.compositions). Reproduces sporadically from Task 0001 onward on main and PR branches. CI passes the same invocation; CI is authoritative. Task 0004 verifier observed it DID NOT reproduce on that run — flaky local cache, not a code issue.
